texto: Moreover, the sciences are monuments devoted to the public good; each citizen owes to them a tribute proportional to his talents. While the great men, carried to the summit of the edifice, draw and put up the higher floors, the ordinary artists scattered in the lower floors, or hidden in the obscurity of the foundations, must only seek to improve what cleverer hands have created.
explicacao: Orientação direta de Charles-Augustin de Coulomb sobre temas ligados a desenvolvimento pessoal. A afirmação “Moreover, the sciences are monuments devoted to the public good; each citizen owes to them a trib…” funciona como lembrete prático ligado a desenvolvimento-pessoal, art, citizen, útil em momentos de citizen e coulomb.
